Overview

This page is a consolidated sales enablement handbook for GTM teams working with technical buyers evaluating Redwood Inference for conversational products. It couples user journey maps (developer and product-owner perspectives) with empirical price-sensitivity test templates and an evidence-backed competitive brief. The goal is to equip Sales Engineers, Solutions Architects, and AEs with repeatable conversation scripts, testable pricing scenarios, and clear security FAQ answers for prospect diligence.

Use cases in scope
- Real-time chatbots (sub-200ms token latency targets)
- Multi-turn assistants with long context (up to 64k tokens)
- High-throughput embedding and reranking pipelines
- Edge / hybrid deployments for regulated customers

Quick reference: what to carry to a technical call
1) Two-slide technical narrative (latency/cost tradeoffs + fallback story)

Summary:
This compendium is an engineer-first sales reference for pre-sales, solutions engineers, and account teams. It combines: concise positioning, conversation scripts tuned for technical audiences, competitive talking points, three pragmatic pricing scenarios with unit economics, a compact case study narrative suitable for slide inclusion, and a targeted security FAQ for technical and security reviewers. Use the conversation scripts verbatim or adapt as appropriate for the buyer persona.

Redwood Inference is a production-first LLM inference platform that gives platform and ML engineers consistent latency, predictable unit cost, and deployment flexibility (hosted, dedicated, private) without rebuilding the serving stack. This dossier is a single-page commercial reference for technical partners (system integrators, ISVs, and channel partners) who co-sell or embed Redwood Inference. It bundles: concise positioning and messaging tailored to technical buyers, a short competitive brief, partner-facing pricing scenarios, a turn-key pilot and onboarding checklist, an outline for a partner-facing pitch deck, two anonymized case narratives, and a curated security FAQ that frequently appears during partner diligence. Use this during joint sales calls, partner enablement sessions, and pilot scoping.

Overview

Purpose: This single-page deliverable is a compact, ready-to-present kit for Sales Architects and Solutions Engineers to use in discovery, technical demos, and negotiation calls. It bundles concise positioning, battle-tested win themes, a short competitive brief, three pricing scenarios with math, a one-paragraph customer case study, a pilot-to-production checklist, and a security FAQ tailored for prospect technical reviewers. Use this when you have 30–60 minutes with an engineer, platform lead, or security reviewer.

How to use this pack

1) Start with the 2-slide narrative (covered in "Deck outline"). 2) Run a 10-minute demo focusing on the model routing and cost controls. 3) Present the pricing scenarios and recommend a pilot path. 4) Close technical questions with the Security FAQ and the Pilot Checklist.

text:
## Purpose
This page provides approved, customer-ready answers and objection handling for Redwood Hosted API pricing and usage measurement.

Scope: Hosted API (chat/text generation, embeddings, reranking). For Dedicated/Private pricing and GPU-seconds, use Dedicated collateral.

**Golden rule:** explain clearly, do not speculate about root cause of discrepancies, and do not promise credits/refunds outside the defined process.

## Quick positioning (use this verbatim)
**Redwood charges based on measured usage (tokens and/or request units) with transparent reporting.** We expose usage at the same granularity we use for billing, and we’ve built safeguards for retries, streaming, and caching so customers can predict spend.

## Definitions (internal alignment + customer-safe phrasing)
### Billed tokens vs raw tokens
- **Billed tokens:** Tokens counted for billing after applying billing rul...

## Purpose
This page contains **approved, customer-facing** copy/paste answers for high-frequency controls:
- **SSO / SAML**
- **SCIM (provisioning/deprovisioning)**
- **RBAC / least privilege**

text:
h1. Purpose
This page defines Redwood Inference’s *Security & Trust messaging pillars* for enterprise buyers. It is designed to keep Sales, Solutions Engineering, and Marketing aligned on:
- *What we emphasize* (pillars and proof points)
- *What we avoid* (competitive-safe language; no roadmap promises)
- *How to tailor* messaging by deployment mode (Hosted API, Dedicated, Private)

This framework is intentionally product-accurate and “competitive-safe”: it highlights how we work and how customers can validate controls, without overstating certifications, making universal residency claims, or implying capabilities that vary by deployment mode.

*Last reviewed:* 2026-02-21
*Owner:* Product Marketing (Camila Reyes) + Security/GRC for factual claims

---

## Purpose
This page defines the approved positioning and copy guidelines for Redwood Inference’s AWS Marketplace and Google Cloud Marketplace listings and supporting sales/customer-facing materials.

Use this as the source of truth for:
- Marketplace listing copy (headline, long description, feature bullets, FAQs)

text:
## Purpose
This one-pager is an internal guide for AEs/SEs/CS to:
- Decide when to route a buyer to AWS/GCP Marketplace vs a direct contract
- Navigate the mechanics (subscription → entitlement → org provisioning → usage/metering)
- Handle the most common procurement and security objections

This doc assumes Redwood’s Marketplace listings align to: **Hosted (API)**, **Dedicated (reserved capacity)**, **Private (VPC/on-prem)** plus optional **add-ons** (e.g., compliance/retention).

---

## When Marketplace is the right path (route to AWS/GCP)
Route a prospect/customer to Marketplace when one or more of the following is true:
1) **Procurement mandate:** “We must purchase via AWS/GCP Marketplace.”
2) **Cloud commit / drawdown:** Customer wants spend to count toward AWS/GCP commitments.
3) **Faster vendor onboarding:** Marketplace reduces supplier onboarding steps (vendor setup, PO flow)...

## Purpose and how to use this FAQ
This page provides approved, customer-facing answers to common enterprise security and compliance questions for **Redwood Inference** across:
- **Hosted API** (multi-tenant managed service)
- **Dedicated** (single-tenant reserved capacity operated by Redwood)
- **Private** (customer VPC and/or on-prem deployment options)

**Sales/SE guidance:**
- Use this language directly in RFPs and questionnaires. Do not improvise claims about certifications, pen test frequency, or residency guarantees.
- If a question cannot be answered with confidence, follow the **Exceptions + Evidence** process (see links below).
- Any contractual commitments (SLA, breach notification windows, audit rights, retention periods) must be confirmed by **Legal/Finance**.

text:
## Purpose
This page is the canonical “guardrails” reference for Security FAQ and RFP responses. It exists to prevent over-commitment, inconsistent wording, and accidental promises (especially around compliance certifications, data residency, retention, and testing).

**Use this page when:**
- You are answering a security questionnaire or RFP.
- A prospect asks for a “simple yes/no” on a nuanced topic.
- You need approved phrasing that is accurate across **Hosted**, **Dedicated**, and **Private** deployments.

**Do not improvise** security/legal claims. If a question goes beyond the approved language below, use the escalation guidance at the end.

---

## Golden rules (read first)
1) **Describe what exists today; never promise roadmap.** If the customer asks “when,” reply with: “We can’t make commitments outside our product and contract. We can share current capabilities and discuss r...

text:
## Purpose
This page is the canonical spoken script for the Redwood demo. It is designed to:
- Keep every AE/SE-led demo consistent.
- Make transitions and “why it matters” crisp (not a feature tour).
- Provide optional branches by audience (Hosted API, Dedicated capacity, Private/VPC/security).
- Include safe questions to ask live, and safe claims to make.
- Provide timing guidance for 20/30/45 minute variants.

This script is tied to the deck (see related pages). If a live step becomes brittle, switch to **Backup Mode** (screenshots/recording) and keep the narrative intact.

---

## Roles & handoffs
**AE (driver):** framing, qualification, business value, next steps.

**SE (driver):** live demo steps, architecture explanations, reliability/economics levers, technical Q&A.

**Handoff cues are explicitly marked** below as:
- **[AE]** to speak
- **[SE]** to speak

## Purpose
This page defines Redwood’s **standard demo personas** and the **mapped, known-good demo scenarios** each persona cares about. It is used to:
- Keep AE/SE-run demos consistent (same story beats, same proof points)
- Choose the correct “happy path” and “objection path” in real time
- Reduce brittleness by reusing a frozen scenario set (model pins, seeded data, stable dashboards)

**Scope:** Live demo + narrative/screenshot modules for enterprise topics. All data shown must be **synthetic**.

---

## Demo personas (who’s watching and what they need)
We typically have 1–2 primary viewers plus a few observers. Use the discovery questions below to identify the primary persona and run the corresponding path.

text:
## Purpose
This page is the demo-ready security FAQ for AEs/SEs. It provides **short, consistent answers** plus **internal links** to deeper references for follow-ups.

**Scope:** Hosted API, Dedicated, and Private/VPC (narrative + screenshots). This is not a replacement for Security review.

**Golden rule for demos:** the demo tenant contains **synthetic data only**. Do not enter customer data, keys, or confidential documents into the demo tenant.

---

## Safe-sharing rules (what you may show live)
**OK to show live (default):**
- Console screens that contain **synthetic demo activity only** (usage charts, latency/cost breakdowns, traces with synthetic payloads)
- RBAC/role screens (tenant roles, project roles) as long as they show demo users only
- Audit log UI **at a high level** (event types, timestamps, actor IDs) with demo users only

text:
## Purpose
These guidelines define *what we say* (and what we avoid) in lifecycle touchpoints for the **Redwood Hosted API** onboarding flow, including:
- Onboarding email sequences (drips)
- In-console onboarding nudges and checklists
- Retargeting ads and landing-page messaging (as applicable)

This page exists to keep messaging:
- Consistent across channels (email, console, ads)
- Accurate and compliant (no unverified claims)
- Aligned to the sprint goals: reduce time-to-first-token (TTFT), lift activation, and improve early retention

## Audience + context
**Primary audience:** software engineers / ML engineers evaluating Redwood Hosted API.

**User state:** brand new to Redwood; often blocked by one of:
- Not creating an API key
- Not knowing the minimum working request
- Confusion about models / parameters
- Early errors (missing/invalid key, region, rate limits)

title: Demo Competitive Objections Cheat Sheet (v1)
preview:
## Purpose

**Principle:** Don’t litigate benchmarks. Anchor on *production economics* (cost/latency/reliability), *de...
text:
## Purpose
This one-pager helps AEs/SEs handle common competitor comparisons during the standard Redwood demo. It is designed for live calls: quick diagnosis, a crisp response, and a clean pivot back to value.

**Principle:** Don’t litigate benchmarks. Anchor on *production economics* (cost/latency/reliability), *deployment flexibility*, and *operational tooling*.

---
## Competitive set (use categories, not vendor-by-vendor)
**A) General cloud AI platforms** (managed endpoints + infra)
- Strong when a customer already standardizes on a single cloud and wants basic managed hosting.
- Common gaps: cross-environment consistency, fine-grained inference economics controls, and transparent per-route cost/latency breakdowns.

**B) LLM API providers** (simple API access)
- Strong for fast prototyping and “good enough” reliability.
- Common gaps: dedicated isolation/throughput guarantees, con...

title: CedarBank (Fintech) - Customer Case Study (Q4 2025)

## Summary
CedarBank is a digital bank focused on SMB lending. This customer story describes how Redwood Inference helped the team power AI-assisted customer support and internal knowledge workflows with predictable performance.

**Customer:** CedarBank
**Industry segment:** Fintech

- More dependable AI experiences during seasonal traffic shifts
- Better visibility into performance and cost drivers
- Easier cross-team standardization of AI features

## Seller notes
Use when a buyer asks about budgeting predictability and operational maturity.

text:
## Purpose
To make it possible to report on and compare customer stories, all published customer story assets must use consistent industry tags and publication markers.

## Scope
Applies to:
- Confluence case studies in: sales-enablement/case-studies/
- Google Drive one-pagers in: shared_drives/go-to-market/customer-stories/

## Required markers (for reporting)
Every published customer story must include:
- `published`
- A quarter marker like `q4-2025`
- Exactly one industry marker like `industry-fintech`

## Allowed industry segments (choose exactly one)
- `industry-fintech`
- `industry-healthcare`
- `industry-ecommerce`
- `industry-legal`
- `industry-saas`
- `industry-industrial`
- `industry-media`

## Definition: Published
"Published" means the asset is approved for external sharing by Marketing or Sales Enablement. Drafts and internal-only narratives should not carry the `publishe...

text:
## Purpose
This page is the internal version of the launch deck for the **Latency/$ Benchmark Report**. It is the canonical talk track for Sales, SE, CS, and Marketing when walking prospects through the story and demoing the interactive benchmark page.

**What this deck is for**
- 10–15 minute overview + “what to do next” CTAs
- A repeatable demo path for the interactive benchmark page
- Audience segmentation guidance (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private)
- Approved language + guardrails for comparative discussion

**What this deck is *not***
- A raw data dump (use the dataset export + methodology appendix)
- A promise of universal performance for every workload (see caveats)

---

## How to use this deck (recommended flows)
**Flow A (Outbound / first call, 8–10 min)**
1) Slides 1–2: Why we published this
2) Slide 4: What Latency/$ means
3) Slide 6: Top findings (1–2 headlines)
4) Slide 10...

text:
## Purpose
This playbook defines how AEs/SDRs/SEs/CSMs should follow up on product-led growth (PLG) signals that indicate a self-serve customer is a strong candidate for a sales-assisted upgrade to **Redwood Dedicated** or **Redwood Private**.

The goal is to:
- Respond quickly to real need (scale, reliability, security/compliance) without spamming customers.
- Standardize messaging and discovery so we qualify consistently.
- Ensure clean handoffs to SE and CS and consistent outcome logging for attribution.

**Pilot scope (Phase 2):** NA self-serve cohort only; HubSpot task-based execution (no automated sequencing beyond task templates).

---

## Quick start (what to do when you get a PLG Signal task)
1) **Open the HubSpot Company record → “PLG Signals” section** and read:
 - Latest signal type(s) and score
 - The “Why routed” summary (top contributing metrics)
 - Suppression history ...

text:
## Purpose
This page is a sales + solutions engineering (SE) friendly security FAQ for self-serve (PLG) accounts that are considering an upgrade to **Redwood Dedicated** or **Redwood Private**.

It is optimized for common “security checklist” questions we see during expansions triggered by scale, reliability, or compliance needs.

**How to use:**
- If you can answer from this page: answer directly and offer to share the Security Overview / SOC 2 report via the approved process.
- If the question hits the **Escalation** section, loop in Security and/or SE early.

---

## Quick positioning (one paragraph you can reuse)
Redwood Inference is built for production inference with enterprise-grade security controls. Customers can start with the Hosted API and upgrade to Dedicated or Private for stronger isolation, network controls, data residency requirements, and compliance needs. Across all...

title: Upgrade triggers: Hosted → Dedicated / Private (pricing + packaging guidance)

## Purpose

It also includes **pricing/packaging guardrails** and approved ...
text:
## Purpose
This page provides clear guidance for when to recommend an upgrade from **Redwood Hosted API** to **Redwood Dedicated** or **Redwood Private**, based on usage thresholds and common pain signals (latency, rate limits, reliability, compliance). It also includes **pricing/packaging guardrails** and approved **discount/credit escalation paths**.

This doc is used by AEs, SDRs, SEs, and CSMs during the PLG-to-sales motion. It is designed to reduce inconsistent recommendations and prevent over-discounting or pushing Dedicated/Private when Hosted is still the right fit.

## Quick decision tree (first pass)
Use this as the default mapping before deep discovery.

1) **Compliance / data controls required?**
- If the customer requires any of the following as a gating requirement: **VPC-only networking**, **customer-managed keys (KMS/HSM)**, **custom retention/audit evidence**, **data ...

text:
Purpose
This page is the internal outline for the “Regulated Workloads” ABM enablement deck used in outbound, discovery, and early-stage evaluation calls for Dedicated and Private deployments.

This is an outline (slide-by-slide) intended for:
- AEs/SEs running 30–45 minute discovery + demo sessions
- Follow-up “security posture + architecture” meetings with CISO/GRC/Platform Engineering
- Executive sponsor syncs when a regulated buyer needs confidence on deployment flexibility, auditability, and operational readiness

Important messaging guardrails (read before using)
- Do not over-claim compliance. Redwood provides controls and evidence to support customer compliance programs; we do not certify customer compliance outcomes.
- Avoid “HIPAA-certified/HIPAA-ready” phrasing. Use: “supports HIPAA-aligned deployments” and “PHI-adjacent workloads” where appropriate, and route detailed clai...

The pilot is designed to validate repeatable positioning + outbound motions for security- and...

## 1) Summary
This document is the internal brief for Redwood Inference’s 6–8 week Vertical ABM pilot focused on **regulated workloads**, initially targeting **Financial Services** and **Healthcare/PHI-adjacent** workloads. The pilot is designed to validate repeatable positioning + outbound motions for security- and compliance-driven buyers, create durable vertical assets, and generate qualified pipeline for **Redwood Dedicated** and **Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem)**.

**Primary outcome:** a scalable playbook (assets + talk tracks + sequences + governance) that reliably produces meetings, SQLs, and POCs for regulated buyers without over-claiming on compliance.

---

## 2) Objectives (what “success” looks like)
### 2.1 Pilot goals
1. **Validate positioning + outbound motion** for compliance-driven buyers (security-first discovery, evidence pack flow, and technical validation path).
2. ...

text:
## Purpose
This FAQ equips Sales, SE, and Security reviewers with consistent language for customer conversations about Redwood Inference’s **SIEM-ready audit export (Beta)**.

**What it is:** A feature-flagged capability that exports Redwood audit events to customer-controlled destinations for security monitoring, compliance evidence, and incident response.

**Beta integrations (initial):**
- **Splunk HEC** (HTTP Event Collector)
- **Datadog Logs intake**
- **Amazon S3 delivery** (object storage delivery for downstream ingest)

**Important:** This is a **beta** feature. See “Beta limitations and disclaimers” below for required customer-facing caveats.

---

## Summary you can use in customer-facing language (approved)
Redwood can export audit events to a customer’s SIEM/log platform (Splunk, Datadog, or S3) with **at-least-once delivery**. Events include a stable **event_id** to suppo...

text:
h1. Overview
This FAQ is an enablement aid for Sales, Solutions Engineering, and Security Engineering support during enterprise security reviews.

*What it covers*
* RBAC v2 scope model (org/project/environment/cluster)
* Least-privilege and separation of duties
* SSO/SAML interaction (authentication vs authorization)
* Auditability: authorization decision logging + SIEM export
* Typical questionnaire responses and recommended customer configurations

*What it is not*
* This is not a contract/SLA document. Confirm any customer-specific commitments with Security & Compliance and Legal.

_Last verified_: 2025-03-21 (Security & Compliance + Product)

h1. Executive summary (talk track)
Redwood Inference provides *fine-grained, deny-by-default RBAC* across the Redwood API, Console, and Control Plane, with *scoped permissions* and *explicit role bindings*. Authorization decisions are *audit...

text:
## Purpose
This page provides standardized, customer-friendly answers (for AEs/SEs/CS) to common enterprise security questionnaire items related to (1) retention controls and (2) Redwood’s baseline Compliance Reporting Pack.

**How to use:** Copy/paste answers as-is when possible. If a customer requests deviations (e.g., shorter TTLs, custom report formatting, on-prem evidence), confirm feasibility with Security & Compliance before committing.

**Owners:** GRC (Olga Petrov) for compliance pack content; Security Engineering (Marcus Lin / Kevin Osei) for audit logs & exports; Product (Naomi Feldman) for roadmap/packaging.

---
## Definitions (use these terms in customer communications)
- **Retention policy / TTL:** Configuration that determines how long a given data class is retained before it is purged (deleted) according to defined semantics.
- **Purge semantics:** Deletion is perform...

title: Hosted Data Residency Enforcement (Region Pinning) — Sales & Security FAQ
preview:
## Purpose
This FAQ equips Sales, Solutions Engineering, and Security reviewers to answer common questions about **Hosted data residency enforcement** (also referred to as **region pinning**) for the Redwood Hosted API, and how behavior differs for **Dedicated** and **Private** deployments.

This document is intende...
text:
## Purpose
This FAQ equips Sales, Solutions Engineering, and Security reviewers to answer common questions about **Hosted data residency enforcement** (also referred to as **region pinning**) for the Redwood Hosted API, and how behavior differs for **Dedicated** and **Private** deployments.

This document is intended for internal enablement. You may quote approved excerpts to customers, but do not forward the full page externally.

---

## Executive summary (customer-facing)
- Customers can configure an **allowed region set** (e.g., **EU-only**) and a **primary region** for Redwood Hosted API processing.
- Redwood enforces residency **at request time** with deterministic checks. If a request would violate policy, it is **blocked** with a consistent error response (no silent cross-region processing).
- **Cross-region fallback is disabled by default** when residency pinning is enabled. ...
